required_skills: Applied machine learning model development including supervised, unsupervised, and self-supervised learning methods
Statistical modelling and probabilistic reasoning including Bayesian methods, regression, and time series analysis
Designing and conducting rigorous A/B experiments and online controlled trials for product or scientific hypotheses
Natural language processing (NLP) and large language model (LLM) fine-tuning and evaluation
Feature engineering, model selection, and hyperparameter optimisation for production-grade ML systems
Deep learning architecture design including CNNs, RNNs, Transformers, and graph neural networks (GNNs)
Scientific Python programming (NumPy, SciPy, Pandas) for data analysis and modelling workflows
Model evaluation, interpretability, and fairness analysis including SHAP values and LIME explanations
Translating ambiguous business or research problems into well-defined scientific problem formulations
Cross-functional collaboration with engineering, product, and data teams to deploy science into production systems
preferred_skills: Experience with reinforcement learning (RL) or multi-armed bandit algorithms for decision optimisation
Knowledge of causal inference and uplift modelling for treatment effect estimation
Familiarity with computer vision techniques including object detection, segmentation, and image generation
Experience with recommendation systems, ranking algorithms, and information retrieval methods
Knowledge of federated learning and privacy-preserving machine learning techniques
Familiarity with MLOps practices including model monitoring, drift detection, and retraining pipelines
Experience with domain-specific applied science in healthcare, climate, materials, or logistics applications
responsibilities: Formulate ambiguous scientific and business problems as well-defined machine learning or statistical modelling tasks, defining success metrics, evaluation frameworks, and baseline benchmarks
Develop, train, and evaluate machine learning models across supervised, unsupervised, and self-supervised paradigms, iterating rapidly from prototype to production-ready implementation
Design and execute online controlled experiments (A/B tests) and offline retrospective analyses to quantify the impact of model interventions on key product and scientific metrics
Apply NLP and large language model techniques including prompt engineering, instruction fine-tuning, and RLHF to develop language understanding and generation systems for applied domains
Build robust feature engineering pipelines and apply automated feature selection methods to improve model signal quality across structured, unstructured, and time-series data sources
Conduct model interpretability and fairness analyses using SHAP, LIME, and counterfactual explanation methods to ensure model transparency and compliance with responsible AI standards
Collaborate with ML engineers and data platform teams to operationalise models into scalable production systems, defining serving requirements, monitoring strategies, and retraining triggers
Perform comprehensive literature reviews and rapid prototyping of state-of-the-art methods to assess their applicability to internal applied science problems and opportunities
Communicate scientific findings, modelling results, and strategic recommendations to technical and non-technical stakeholders through written reports, dashboards, and executive presentations
Contribute to the applied science community through publication, open-source contributions, conference presentations, and internal knowledge-sharing seminars
